sources.label: StockTitan - Dycom (DY) news feed (Q1 FY27 record results, NTI acquisition, raised FY27 guidance - all pre-June 6)
SEC 8-K - Dycom fiscal Q1 2027 earnings release ($1.965B revenue +56.1% YoY, $11.9B backlog +46.5%)
StockAnalysis - DY analyst forecast (Strong Buy consensus, avg PT ~$637 after KeyBanc $610 / BofA $650 / Cantor $654 raises)

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at rank 62. No new information since June 6; the blowout Q1 FY27 print, NTI deal, and sell-side PT raises were already absorbed in yesterday's down move, and the ~10-16% flow-driven retrace from the post-earnings spike ($535 to $447, -2.64% today) with an intact $11.9B backlog modestly restores asymmetry rather than justifying another cut.
verdicts.bull: Record backlog of $11.9B (+46.5%) and 24.7% organic growth directly validate the fiber-backhaul/data-center interconnect thesis, and the pullback to $447 vs analyst PTs of $610-654 rebuilds upside that the post-earnings spike had consumed.
verdicts.neutral: The name has been discovered: Strong Buy consensus and three major PT raises mean the latent-node premise is largely spent, and at +28.5% YTD with a 13.4% EBITDA-margin services multiple, returns from here track execution, not rerating.
verdicts.bear: Labor-intensive construction economics leave margins exposed to wage inflation and project timing; a carrier-capex or AI-datacenter-build pause would compress the backlog-to-revenue conversion the rerated multiple now assumes, and the $275M NTI plus prior $1.95B electrical-contractor deals add integration risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 FY27 results ($1.94-2.01B guided) and continued data-center interconnect/inside-plant award announcements; near-term sell-side touchpoints at the Stifel Cross Sector and Guggenheim fireside events.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating crowding: with sell-side PTs at $610-654 and the stock fully covered, any backlog-growth deceleration or margin miss triggers a sharp de-rating of a now-consensus name.
